productionMasterPR.role: exact candidate and Production Master contract source

ancestry.rule: This review pack descends directly from exact PR #59. PR #55 is a read-only board-focused screenshot source and contributes no ancestry or runtime code.

candidate.immutabilityRule: Every composite uses decoded pixels from this exact SHA. The source file is not cropped, retouched, repainted, redesigned, regenerated, or overwritten.

allowedOperations: alpha composite over a review-only background
add review labels, slot rectangles, and metadata outside the candidate pixels
forbiddenOperations: pose, identity, face, eye, hair, hood, ear, costume, bow, color, or proportion change
candidate source crop, retouch, repaint, or overwrite
runtime, portrait, icon, static fallback, or motion replacement

resampling.note: Point-filter insets magnify the already reduced review result; they do not create an alternate candidate.

observations: Face, large green eyes, hood, hair locks, pointed ears, bow, costume, and full silhouette are available for one-to-one inspection.
The source margins remain visible: left 71 px, top 125 px, right 68 px, bottom 105 px.
The bow tip, quiver, hair, cape, and both feet are uncropped; the outer canvas border remains transparent.
concerns: The technical pass alone did not grant approval; the user's later exact-file approval is recorded in the dedicated approval record.

method.inspectionInset: three-times point magnification of the 98x147 reduced result

observations: The hood-and-ear head shape, green/gold palette, and oversized bow make the Ranger class read immediately against warm sand and stone.
The all-alpha silhouette is approximately 82x118 px and remains within one 98 px tile width; feet are anchored inside the highlighted tile.
The bow remains a separate readable arc and does not merge into the body.
The face and two-eye pattern remain locatable at context scale; fine iris, leather, and gold micro-detail intentionally collapse.
concerns: Green hood and hair become one dominant mass at native board scale; gold hood trim and skin/eye contrast currently preserve separation.
This is a screenshot composite, not a browser/runtime validation and not permission to change tile, camera, or unit geometry.

method.inspectionInset: four-times point magnification of the 80x120 reduced result

observations: The all-alpha silhouette is approximately 68x97 px, leaving horizontal space inside a 98 px bench slot.
The complete hood, ears, bow, quiver, cape, legs, and feet remain uncropped.
The bow and green/gold block remain the strongest readable class cues.
concerns: Face and individual eye detail are marginal at native bench scale; the four-times inset is diagnostic and must not be mistaken for the native read.
Hair and hood compress further than at board scale. Gold trim and the face opening must remain intact in any future export.
No slot size, anchor, or gameplay geometry change is authorized to solve a visual concern.

method.background: deterministic review-only card primitives; no production UI asset

observations: The full-silhouette layout keeps face and eyes dominant while retaining the entire bow, hair, cape, legs, and feet.
The Archer/Ranger identity is immediate through hood, ears, green/gold palette, bow, and quiver.
The bow sits beside the face rather than covering it.
concerns: The demonstrated tight crop touches the viewport bottom and clips the feet/lower bow silhouette; it is explicitly not recommended.
Card frame, title, rarity, cost, and UI remain separate presentation ownership and must not be baked into the approved character master.

approvalDecision.technicalSummary: Full-size and safe shop-card framing pass technical review. Board scale passes with expected fine-detail loss. Bench scale remains usable but has the highest face/hood-hair compression risk.
completedApprovalEvidence: user reviewed the exact candidate and all four review contexts
user explicitly approved the candidate identified by SHA-256
this approval-only change records neutralMasterApproved=true without modifying candidate bytes
approvalDecision.approvalBoundary: Approval comes from the explicit user decision. It approves only the exact Neutral Master source; canonicalApproved and runtimeEligible remain false, and no derivative or motion package is approved.

protectedAreas: camera, board, bench, and map geometry
